trajectory_summary: This is a no-contact call. The rep dialed, announced their name, checked for a live connection twice over 12 seconds, and received no customer response. No qualification was attempted, no appointment was booked, and no customer voice appears in the transcript. There is nothing to coach on persuasion, objection handling, or protocol adherence — the only coachable surface is what the rep did (or did not do) after the line went dead.
outcome_dispute: No v1 outcome tag was provided with this call. Based on the transcript, the correct CRM classification is no-contact / zero-engagement — no appointment booked, no customer voice recorded, no qualification attempted.

primary_coaching_focus.why: The transcript ends at 12 seconds with no customer engagement and no audible recovery action. The only coachable question on this call is what the rep did after the line went dead — voicemail left, callback logged, lead dispositioned in CRM. If the rep has no consistent no-contact recovery protocol, that is a comp risk on paid leads and a volume leak on all lead types. This is the one conversation worth having with this rep about this call.

key_moments.stewart_read: The rep delivered the name-identification component of the intro correctly. On a 12-second no-contact call, the stronger hypothesis is that the rep was mid-dial and intended to complete the full intro once the customer answered — not that the rep was unprepared or had abandoned the script. The alternative hypothesis (rep was not ready with the full intro) is weaker because the rep did produce the correct first element without hesitation. Coaching on intro readiness is a low-stakes precautionary note, not the primary read.

The rep checked for a live connection after silence, which is correct dead-air procedure. No coaching gap here. The gap that cannot be confirmed from the transcript is what happened after the second 'Hello?' at 00:12 — specifically, whether the rep left a voicemail or logged a callback action.

This is the last audible rep line. No voicemail script, no callback commitment, and no disposition note appear in the transcript. The absence of a recovery action after a no-contact is the only coachable surface on this call. If the rep has a consistent pattern of no-contact calls without documented follow-up, that is a volume and comp risk — not a persuasion gap.

The call never reached any required beat beyond the rep's name identification. No verify, no bill collection, no button-up — not because the rep skipped them, but because the customer never engaged. The call shape is structurally incomplete by circumstance, not by rep choice. The only question worth asking is whether the rep's post-no-contact behavior (voicemail, CRM log, callback scheduling) met Ion's standard. That answer lives in the CRM, not in this transcript.
